title: Check if Geometric
text: A geometric series has a constant ratio between consecutive terms. The summand $$0.76k+1$$ is linear in k — consecutive terms differ by $$.76$$, not by a constant ratio. This is arithmetic.

title: Can an arithmetic series have an infinite sum?
text: Arithmetic series with non-zero common difference diverge — the terms do not shrink to zero.

title: Conclusion
text: Because this is an arithmetic series, the infinite sum is not defined.
